title: What is the difference between process and thread in OS?
options: Process: independent memory space, heavy creation (fork), isolated. Thread: shared memory within process, lightweight, needs synchronization. Threads within a process share heap but have separate stacks. Context switching is faster for threads
correct_answer: Process: independent memory space, heavy creation (fork), isolated. Thread: shared memory within process, lightweight, needs synchronization. Threads within a process share heap but have separate stacks. Context switching is faster for threads

title: What is consistent hashing?
options: Maps both keys and nodes to a ring (hash space); each key is assigned to the nearest node clockwise. Adding/removing a node only remaps K/N keys (K=total keys, N=nodes) instead of rehashing everything. Used in distributed caches, CDNs
correct_answer: Maps both keys and nodes to a ring (hash space); each key is assigned to the nearest node clockwise. Adding/removing a node only remaps K/N keys (K=total keys, N=nodes) instead of rehashing everything. Used in distributed caches, CDNs

title: Implement Prim's minimum spanning tree algorithm.
What is the CAP theorem?
description: Explain the CAP theorem in distributed systems.
options: In a distributed system, you can only guarantee 2 of 3: Consistency (all nodes see same data), Availability (every request gets a response), Partition tolerance (system works despite network failures). Since partitions are unavoidable, choose CP or AP
correct_answer: In a distributed system, you can only guarantee 2 of 3: Consistency (all nodes see same data), Availability (every request gets a response), Partition tolerance (system works despite network failures). Since partitions are unavoidable, choose CP or AP

title: Implement a rate limiter using token bucket algorithm.
description: Write a token bucket rate limiter.
correct_answer:
import time

class TokenBucket:
    def __init__(self, capacity, refill_rate):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.tokens = capacity
        self.refill_rate = refill_rate  # tokens per second
        self.last_refill = time.time()

    def _refill(self):
        now = time.time()
        elapsed = now - self.last_refill
        self.tokens = min(self.capacity, self.tokens + elapsed * self.refill_rate)
        self.last_refill = now

    def allow_request(self, tokens=1):
        self._refill()
        if self.tokens >= tokens:
            self.tokens -= tokens
            return True
        return False

title: What is the difference between horizontal and vertical scaling?
options: Vertical (scale up): add more CPU/RAM/disk to one machine; simpler but has hardware limits. Horizontal (scale out): add more machines; more complex but virtually unlimited. Horizontal needs load balancers, data partitioning, and distributed systems
correct_answer: Vertical (scale up): add more CPU/RAM/disk to one machine; simpler but has hardware limits. Horizontal (scale out): add more machines; more complex but virtually unlimited. Horizontal needs load balancers, data partitioning, and distributed systems

title: What is a bloom filter?
description: How does a Bloom filter work?
options: Probabilistic data structure: uses k hash functions mapping to a bit array. Insert: set k bits. Lookup: check k bits — all set means 'probably yes', any unset means 'definitely no'. No false negatives, possible false positives. O(k) time, very space-efficient
correct_answer: Probabilistic data structure: uses k hash functions mapping to a bit array. Insert: set k bits. Lookup: check k bits — all set means 'probably yes', any unset means 'definitely no'. No false negatives, possible false positives. O(k) time, very space-efficient

title: What is the difference between REST and GraphQL?
description: Compare REST and GraphQL API design.
options: REST: multiple endpoints, fixed response shape, over/under-fetching common, HTTP caching easy. GraphQL: single endpoint, client specifies exact data needed, no over-fetching, batches requests, strongly typed schema. Choose based on use case complexity
correct_answer: REST: multiple endpoints, fixed response shape, over/under-fetching common, HTTP caching easy. GraphQL: single endpoint, client specifies exact data needed, no over-fetching, batches requests, strongly typed schema. Choose based on use case complexity

title: Implement a circular buffer (ring buffer).
What is database sharding?
options: Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple database instances; each shard holds a subset of data based on shard key (hash, range, or directory). Improves write scalability and capacity. Challenges: cross-shard queries, rebalancing, transactions
correct_answer: Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple database instances; each shard holds a subset of data based on shard key (hash, range, or directory). Improves write scalability and capacity. Challenges: cross-shard queries, rebalancing, transactions
